nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Verney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Varney
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from a French place called Vernay, probably chiefly Saint-Paul-de-Vernay (Calvados). The placename comes from a derivative of Gaulish verno- ‘alder’ + the locative suffix -acum.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Richard de Verney was born in 1221, in Madley, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of Sir Henry de Verney. He had at least 2 sons. He died in 1276, in his hometown, at the age of 55.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard de Verney est né en 1221, à Madley, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de Sir Henry de Verney. Il a eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1276, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 55 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Richard de Verney wurde 1221 in Madley, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Sir Henry de Verney geboren. Er hatte mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1276 im Alter von 55 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Richard de Verney è nato il nel 1221 nel Madley, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di Sir Henry de Verney. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1276, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 55 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Verneyは1221年にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアマドリーでSir Henry de Verneyの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼は1276年に故郷で55歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Verney(은)는 1221년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 매들리에서 Sir Henry de Verney의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1276년 그의 고향에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard de Verney nasceu em 1221, em Madley, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filho de Sir Henry de Verney. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1276, em sua cidade natal, com 55 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard de Verney родился в 1221 г. в местности мадли, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания. Его отца звали Sir Henry de Verney. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1276 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 55 лет.
